steps.title: Use corresponding parts of similar triangles
steps.content: In similar triangles, corresponding angles are congruent. Corresponding side lengths are proportional, but they are not necessarily equal unless the scale factor is 1.

steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: In similar triangles, corresponding angles are always equal. Since $J$ corresponds to $M$, we have $\angle M = \angle J = 34^{\circ}$. Statement I is true.

steps.title: Check the side statement
steps.content: Similar triangles have proportional sides, not necessarily equal sides. $JK$ corresponds to $MN$, but the scale factor is unknown. $MN$ could equal 7, 14, 3.5, or any other positive value. Statement II is not necessarily true.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: I only — Statement I ($\angle M = 34^{\circ}$) must be true; Statement II ($MN = 7$) need not be true. That matches Choice A — I only.
  • Choice B: II only — Statement I is definitely true and Statement II is not guaranteed. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: I and II — Statement II is not necessarily true. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: Neither I nor II — Statement I must be true. Eliminated.
